x-enumeration-literals.name: Sequential Credit Application
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Sequential / Priority-Based Application: Payments or disbursements are applied in a predefined order to different components.

Comment
Example: Repayment applied first to fees, then interest, then principal.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Proportional Credit Application
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Proportional / Split Application: Payments are divided proportionally across multiple components.

Comment:
Example: €100 repayment split 60% to principal, 40% to interest.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Single Component Credit Application
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Full / Single Component Application: Payment applied fully to a single component of the account.

Comment:
Example: Full repayment applied entirely to principal.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Conditional Credit Application
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Funds or repayments applied only if certain conditions are met.

Comment:
Example: Payment applied to overdue principal only if balance exceeds zero.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Automatic Credit Application
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Automatic / System-Driven Application: Allocation occurs automatically according to predefined system rules.

Comment:
Example: Loan repayment automatically allocated by the banking system.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Manual Credit Application
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Account holder specifies how the funds or repayment should be applied.

Comment:
Example: Customer chooses to pay more toward interest or principal in a loan.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Event-Based Credit Application
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Application occurs upon a specific trigger or event.

Comment:
Example: Penalty fee applied automatically when repayment is late.
